sections.content:
The character rotated again (now facing down) and moved right. Looking at the image, the character is now upside down with the red feet at the top. The green square is still there but seems to have been partially absorbed.
Let me check the current state to see how much green remains.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check remaining green and character state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
total_green = 0
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'N':
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Node {node['id']} (N): rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, {node['pixels']}px")
        total_green += node['pixels']
    elif node['color'] == 'W' and node['pixels'] > 100:
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Character (W): rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, {node['pixels']}px")

print(f"\nTotal green pixels: {total_green}")

# Check top bar
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
white_count = sum(1 for c in ascii_lines[0] if c == 'W')
print(f"Top bar white pixels: {white_count}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
